Title: Category - Facet Filtering
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Category with faceted products exists
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any category with facets on {{FRONT_URL}}
[ASSERT] Verify facet panel is visible in sidebar
[ACT] Click on any available facet value (e.g., any option from any facet group)
[WAIT] Wait for product list to update
[ASSERT] Verify products are filtered; result count updates
[ACT] Apply an additional facet value from another group
[WAIT] Wait for product list to update
[ASSERT] Verify combined AND filtering — result set narrower
Assertions:
[DOM] Facet panel visible with multiple facet groups
[DOM] Clicking a facet value filters the product list
[MATH] Multiple facets combine with AND logic — result count after 2 filters <= result count after 1 filter
[DOM] Result count label updates after each filter action
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on filter request
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Spinner >5s after filter click; facet panel not rendering; result count unchanged after filter; console TypeError

Title: Category - Price Range Filter
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Products with varying prices exist in category
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any category page on {{FRONT_URL}}
[ASSERT] Locate price range filter (slider or min/max inputs)
[ACT] Set minimum price value (choose any value above 0 that is below the page maximum)
[ACT] Set maximum price value (choose any value that is above the minimum but below the page maximum)
[ACT] Apply or confirm the price range filter
[WAIT] Wait for product list to update
[ASSERT] Verify all displayed products fall within the specified range
Assertions:
[DOM] Price range slider or input fields present
[MATH] All displayed product prices are within the set min/max range (each price >= min AND <= max)
[DOM] Products outside price range are not shown
[DOM] Result count updates to reflect filtered set (count >= 0)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on price filter request
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Price filter controls not rendering; products outside range still visible; result count unchanged; console TypeError

Title: Category - Clear Filters
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Filters applied to a category page
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any category page on {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Apply multiple filters (e.g., any facet value + price range)
[WAIT] Wait for filtered results to load
[ACT] Click 'Clear all' or 'Reset filters' button
[WAIT] Wait for full product list to restore
[ASSERT] Verify all filters are cleared and full list is shown
Assertions:
[DOM] All filter chips removed from chip bar
[DOM] Full unfiltered product list restored (count > filtered count, or equals if all products matched)
[DOM] Filter sidebar UI reset — no values selected
[STATE] URL cleaned of filter parameters
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on reset request
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Reset button not visible; filters remain after reset; products not restored; URL still contains filter params; console TypeError

Title: Category - Infinite Scroll Loads More Products
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Category with 20+ products. Storefront catalog pagination mode is infinite_scroll on vcst-qa (load_more is the alternative theme-configurable mode).
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a category page with 20+ products on {{FRONT_URL}}
[WAIT] Wait for the initial product grid to render
[ACT] Note the initial visible product-card count
[SCROLL] Scroll to the bottom of the product list
[WAIT] Wait for the next batch of products to auto-load
[ASSERT] Verify more product cards were appended (visible count increased)
[SCROLL] Scroll to the bottom again
[WAIT] Wait for the next batch to auto-load
[ASSERT] Verify additional product cards were appended again
Assertions:
[STATE] Scrolling to the bottom auto-appends the next page of products (visible count grows by roughly one page size each scroll)
[DOM] No numbered pagination, no Prev/Next buttons, and no 'Load more' button on the listing (vcst-qa pagination mode = infinite_scroll)
[STATE] The URL does NOT gain a ?page= query parameter while scrolling
[MATH] Appended products are distinct — no duplicates of already-shown items
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] next-page catalog/search request fires on scroll and returns 200
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Scrolling does not load more products; numbered pagination / Prev-Next / 'Load more' controls appear (would indicate a different mode than infinite_scroll); duplicate products appended; console TypeError

Title: Category - View Mode Toggle & No Per-Page Selector
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Category with many products
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any category page on {{FRONT_URL}}
[WAIT] Wait for the product grid and listing toolbar to render
[ASSERT] Verify the toolbar has NO items-per-page / 'per page' selector (by design — page size is fixed and paging is infinite scroll)
[ASSERT] Verify the toolbar DOES expose a Sort-by dropdown and a Grid/List view toggle
[ACT] Click the view-mode toggle (e.g. 'Switch to list view')
[WAIT] Wait for the layout to re-render
[ASSERT] Verify the product layout switched (grid <-> list)
Assertions:
[STATE] No items-per-page selector exists in the listing toolbar (by design)
[DOM] Sort-by dropdown (combobox) is present in the toolbar
[DOM] Grid/List view toggle is present and switches the product card layout when clicked
[STATE] Switching view mode does not reload the page or lose loaded products
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on view-mode change
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: A per-page selector is expected but the storefront has none (test-vs-design mismatch — absence is correct); view-mode toggle missing or does not change layout; console TypeError

[ASSERT] Verify close (x) button is present on the chip

[DOM] Close (x) button visible on chip
[MATH] Product count updates after chip applied (count > 0 or 0 if no products match)

Title: Filters - Browser Back/Forward Navigation
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Category page with filters
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any category page on {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Apply any filter value A (note the chip label)
[WAIT] Wait for chip and URL param
[ACT] Apply any different filter value B (note the chip label)
[WAIT] Wait for chip and URL param for filter B
[KEY] Press browser Back button
[WAIT] Wait for filter B to be removed
[ASSERT] Verify filter B chip is gone; filter A chip remains
[KEY] Press browser Forward button
[WAIT] Wait for filter B to be restored
[ASSERT] Verify filter B chip reappears
Assertions:
[STATE] Browser back removes the most recently applied filter
[STATE] Browser forward restores the filter
[STATE] Chips sync correctly with browser history navigation
[STATE] URL params match chip state at each step
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on history navigation
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Back button removes all filters instead of just the last; chips not syncing with URL state after history navigation; console TypeError

Title: Facet Filter Badge - WCAG Contrast at xs Size

Assertions:

Title: Range Facet - Non-Price Numeric Property Rendering
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Category with a numeric range property (non-price) configured in Admin > Stores > Aggregation properties; range filter visible on that category page; resolve the category URL and range property name at runtime
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a category page that has a non-price numeric range filter in its facet panel on {{FRONT_URL}} (resolve the category URL at runtime by inspecting the filter bar for any range filter that is not the price range)
[WAIT] Wait for category page and filter bar to load
[ASSERT] Locate the non-price numeric range filter button in the filter bar (e.g., a property like volume, weight, or size)
[ACT] Click the range filter button to open the range dropdown
[WAIT] Wait for slider/range UI to appear
[ASSERT] Verify slider renders with histogram chart (if present), dual slider handles, and min/max input fields
[ASSERT] Note the current min and max values shown in the inputs
[ACT] Enter a value above the minimum in the min input field and press Enter
[WAIT] Wait for product list to update
Assertions:
[DOM] Non-price numeric range filter button is visible in the filter bar
[DOM] Slider renders with range controls (handles and min/max input fields)
[MATH] After filtering, only products within the selected range appear (product count > 0 if valid range applied)
[MATH] Product count matches the number of products within the selected range
[STATE] Filter chip appears for the range filter
[DOM] Products outside the selected range are excluded
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /graphql (products) returns HTTP 200 with the range filter applied
[NETWORK] Response totalCount matches displayed product count
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Range filter button missing from filter bar; slider not rendering; products outside range appearing; range values corrupted in URL; console TypeError

Title: Range Facet - VP-9035 Regression: Underscore Field Name Preserved in Chip
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Category page with an underscore-named numeric property range filter configured (e.g., a property whose name contains an underscore character); VP-9035 fix deployed; resolve the category URL and property name at runtime
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a category page that has a range filter whose field name contains an underscore character on {{FRONT_URL}} (resolve at runtime by inspecting the aggregation properties or filter bar labels)
[WAIT] Wait for filter bar to load
[ACT] Click the underscore-named range filter button
[ACT] Enter a value above the minimum in the min input field and press Enter
[WAIT] Wait for filtered results
[ASSERT] Inspect the URL query parameters — verify the full field name including underscore is preserved (e.g., 'Field_Name' not 'Field')
[ASSERT] Inspect the filter chip text — verify the full field name is preserved in the chip label
[ASSERT] Verify products outside the selected range are excluded
Assertions:
[STATE] URL facets parameter contains the full field name including underscore (not truncated at the underscore character)
[DOM] Filter chip shows the full field name including underscore (not truncated)
[MATH] Result count > 0 and matches products within the selected range
[DOM] Products outside the selected range are excluded
[STATE] No facets URL parameter corruption
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GraphQL request filter param contains the full field name including underscore
[NETWORK] Response aggregations match the full field name
[NETWORK] No mismatched IsApplied states in aggregation response
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Filter chip shows truncated name without underscore; URL mutates to wrong facet range; products outside range visible; GetFieldName splits on underscore

Title: Range Facet - Price Range Chip Display and Removal
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Category page with price range filter available

[ACT] Click close (x) button on the range filter chip
[WAIT] Wait for full product list to restore
Assertions:

[STATE] After chip removal, full unfiltered product list is restored
[MATH] Product count returns to original unfiltered count after chip removal

Cross_Layer_Checks:

[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors

Title: Range Facet - Slider Min Greater Than Max Validation
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Category page with price range slider or min/max input fields
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any category page with price range filter on {{FRONT_URL}}
[WAIT] Wait for filter controls
[ACT] Open price range filter
[ASSERT] Note the current min and max bounds from the page
[ACT] Enter a minimum value that is higher than the maximum value (intentionally inverted range)
[ACT] Attempt to apply the invalid range
[WAIT] Observe UI response
Assertions:
[STATE] System auto-swaps min/max values silently — URL reflects corrected range [lower TO higher] (lower value becomes min, higher value becomes max regardless of which field they were entered in)
[DOM] Slider handles reposition to reflect the corrected range
[DOM] Filter chip shows the corrected range (lower value to higher value)
[DOM] No JS crash or broken UI state
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] No 4xx/5xx triggered by invalid range input
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: JS crash on invalid range; empty page with no error message; corrupted URL params; console TypeError

Title: Range Facet - Combined with Term Facet (AND Logic)
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Category page with both term facets (e.g., Brand or Type) and range facets (price) available
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any category page with multiple filter types on {{FRONT_URL}}
[WAIT] Wait for filter panel to load
[ACT] Apply any term facet filter value
[WAIT] Wait for filtered results; note result count as Count_Brand
[ACT] Apply any price range filter (choose a range that still leaves some products visible)
[WAIT] Wait for combined filtered results; note result count as Count_Combined
[ASSERT] Verify Count_Combined <= Count_Brand
[ASSERT] Verify all displayed products match both the term facet AND price range
[ACT] Remove the price range chip
[WAIT] Wait for results to update
[ASSERT] Verify result count returns to Count_Brand
Assertions:
[MATH] Count_Combined <= Count_Brand (AND logic narrows results)
[DOM] All products after combined filter match the selected term facet AND fall within the price range
[STATE] Removing range filter restores the term-facet-only result count
[DOM] Both chips visible when both filters active
[STATE] Removing range chip preserves term facet chip
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /graphql returns results matching both filter and facet criteria
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Combined filter returning more results than single filter (OR instead of AND); products outside price range appearing with correct facet value; removing range chip also removes term chip; console TypeError

[WAIT] Wait for category page to reload
[ASSERT] Verify range filter is restored from URL

[MATH] Filtered product count is consistent before and after refresh

[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on refresh or back navigation
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Range filter cleared after refresh; chip not rendering after back navigation; URL losing filter params; different product count after refresh; console TypeError

Title: Range Facet - Cascading Bucket Counts Accuracy
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Category page with price range filter showing bucket options (e.g., distinct price ranges with product counts)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any category page with range facet buckets on {{FRONT_URL}}
[WAIT] Wait for filter panel to load
[ASSERT] Locate range facet with bucket options showing product counts
[ASSERT] Note the count for each bucket (each count is a non-negative integer)
[ACT] Apply any term facet filter value
[WAIT] Wait for filtered results and facet counts to update
[ASSERT] Verify range bucket counts have updated (cascading)
[ACT] Click on any range bucket to filter
[WAIT] Wait for filtered results
[ASSERT] Count actual product cards displayed
[ASSERT] Verify card count matches the bucket count shown
Assertions:
[MATH] Range bucket count matches actual number of products returned when that bucket is selected
[MATH] After applying a term facet, range bucket counts are <= original counts (never increase)
[DOM] Each range bucket displays a non-negative count
[STATE] Zero-count buckets are either hidden or shown as (0)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /graphql aggregation counts match displayed bucket counts
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Bucket count shown does not match number of products displayed; bucket counts not updating after term facet applied; zero products returned for non-zero bucket; console TypeError

Title: Range Facet - Currency Switch Updates Price Range Values
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Category page with price range filter; store supports multiple currencies
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any category page with price range filter on {{FRONT_URL}}
[WAIT] Wait for filter panel to load
[ASSERT] Note price range min/max values in default currency
[ACT] Switch currency using the currency selector (select any alternative currency)
[WAIT] Wait for page to reload with new currency
[ASSERT] Verify price range filter min/max values updated to the new currency amounts
[ACT] Apply any price range in the new currency
[WAIT] Wait for filtered results
[ASSERT] Verify all product prices are displayed in the new currency and fall within the selected range
Assertions:
[DOM] Price range filter bounds change after currency switch (actual values differ from default currency values, not just a symbol change)
[MATH] Range min/max in new currency differ from default currency values
[DOM] After range filter in new currency, all visible products show prices within range in the new currency
[STATE] No mixed-currency state in filter controls or product cards
[STATE] Product count may decrease significantly if few products have prices in the new currency — this is expected behavior (BL-PRICE-005)

[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Range values unchanged after currency switch; products showing previous-currency prices; mixed currencies in filter and product cards; filter parameter still using old currency; console TypeError

Title: Range Facet - Open-Ended Range: Up To Maximum
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Category page with price range filter; products at various price points
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any category page with price range filter on {{FRONT_URL}}
[WAIT] Wait for range filter to load
[ASSERT] Note the current price range min and max values from the filter controls
[ACT] Set range with only maximum value (leave min empty or at the page minimum, set max to any value below the page maximum)
[ACT] Apply the filter
[WAIT] Wait for filtered results
[ASSERT] Verify all displayed products are priced at or below the chosen maximum
[ASSERT] Verify products above the chosen maximum are excluded
Assertions:
[MATH] All displayed product prices <= the chosen maximum value
[DOM] No products with price above the maximum visible
[DOM] Filter chip shows an open-ended upper-bound label (e.g., 'price: <= X' with the chosen max value)
[MATH] Product count > 0 (valid range has products) and matches actual products at or below maximum
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GraphQL filter uses open-ended syntax [TO max_value] in URL facets parameter
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Products above maximum appearing in results; filter chip showing wrong range; product count mismatch; console TypeError

Title: Category Permalink — Slug Preserved Without Truncation After EN→DE Locale Switch
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Storefront accessible at {{FRONT_URL}}; EN is the active locale; at least one top-level category is reachable; DE locale is available in the language switcher
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/catalog
[WAIT] Category listing page loads
[ACT] Click the first top-level category link in the category grid
[WAIT] Category page loads and URL settles
[ASSERT] Capture the current URL path segment after {{FRONT_URL}} — store as SLUG_BEFORE (e.g. '/accessories'). Verify SLUG_BEFORE is non-empty and does not start with a language code prefix
[ACT] Open the language/locale selector in the top header (click the language switcher element)
[WAIT] Language selector dropdown or panel opens
[ACT] Select 'Deutsch (de)' from the language list
[WAIT] Page reloads or navigates; URL changes to include the /de/ prefix
[ASSERT] Capture the URL path segment after the /de/ prefix — store as SLUG_AFTER
[ASSERT] Compare SLUG_AFTER to SLUG_BEFORE: they must be identical (same full slug string, zero characters dropped or added)
[ASSERT] Verify the category page renders correctly: no 404, category heading visible
Assertions:
[NAV] URL after locale switch is {{FRONT_URL}}/de/<SLUG_BEFORE> (the full original slug follows /de/ without any character loss)
[STATE] SLUG_AFTER == SLUG_BEFORE (character-for-character equality; length(SLUG_AFTER) == length(SLUG_BEFORE))
[DOM] Category heading is visible and non-empty on the DE-locale page
[DOM] No 404 or 'page not found' error content visible
[DOM] Language indicator in the header shows 'de' or 'Deutsch' as the active locale
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET {{FRONT_URL}}/de/<SLUG_BEFORE> returns HTTP 200 (not 404)
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors during locale switch and page load
Failure_Signals: URL after locale switch is {{FRONT_URL}}/de/stinations instead of {{FRONT_URL}}/de/destinations (slug truncated); 404 on the DE-locale category URL; SLUG_AFTER shorter than SLUG_BEFORE; console TypeError on locale switch; language selector does not include DE option
Cleanup: Switch locale back to EN (click language switcher → select English)
